task_completion.visual_evidence: The main map view shows yellow or white grid lines representing latitude and longitude coordinates.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the correct region (Nova Scotia)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map view is focused on the Atlantic coast of North America, specifically showing the Nova Scotia peninsula.
A 'New Placemark' or 'Google Earth - Edit Placemark' dialog is visible, and the 'Name' field contains exactly '45N Atlantic Landfall'.

task_completion.description: Position the placemark at the 45°N coastline intersection
task_completion.visual_evidence: The placemark pin is visually positioned exactly where the 45°N latitude grid line intersects the coastline of Nova Scotia (likely the eastern shore near 62°W).

task_completion.description: Document the longitude in the description
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Description' field in the open placemark dialog (or the pop-up info balloon) contains text referencing the longitude coordinate (e.g., approx 61.95°W or 62°W).

task_completion.description: Save the placemark to My Places
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Places' sidebar on the left shows the item '45N Atlantic Landfall' listed under the 'My Places' folder.

integrity.description: Verify agent used the software GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard Google Earth interface elements (menus, toolbar, sidebar) rather than a script or terminal output performing the actions.

integrity.description: Verify work was done within the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The placemark and grid are rendered within the 3D map view, not pasted as a static image or overlay from an external source.
The placemark properties/creation dialog is captured open during the process, indicating manual data entry rather than bulk KML import.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target location is where the 45th parallel north (45°N) intersects the eastern Atlantic coast of Nova Scotia, Canada. This occurs at approximately 61.95°W longitude. The placemark must be on the coastline at this latitude. The name must be '45N Atlantic Landfall'.
